description_long: This is a slender, double-edged dagger with a dark, pointed blade and a metallic sheen, suggesting it is made of steel or a similar alloy. The blade is straight and tapers to a fine point, ideal for piercing. The handle is cylindrical, slightly contoured, and appears to be made of a darker, possibly brass or bronze metal, with a subtle patina indicating age or frequent use. A small, circular guard separates the blade from the handle, providing protection for the user's hand. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible ornamentation or decorative features. The object is compact and lightweight, suitable for handheld use in close-quarters situations.
description: A slender, double-edged dagger with a dark metallic blade and a cylindrical, bronze-toned handle separated by a small guard. The design is utilitarian and unadorned.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the double-edged blade and the cylindrical handle with a small guard.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the rounded end of the handle and the narrow profile of the blade.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the blade and handle from the other side.
description_view_3: Front view showing the pointed tip of the blade and the circular cross-section of the handle.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, slightly contoured, metallic, smooth surface
